title: Maintain parked utility recheck policy
goal: Record why Kopia, lazygit, KDE Connect/GSConnect, Zellij, Windmill, Open WebUI, AnythingLLM, Dyad and n8n are parked and what would reopen them.

acceptance.assertion: Each parked tool has a specific reopen condition instead of a vague maybe-later status.
n8n is explicitly listed as deleted/not active and requires a later explicit user decision before any new Bureau task or install path is created.
Windmill/Open WebUI/AnythingLLM/Dyad remain sandbox-only until a boundary review proves they do not create a second control-plane or knowledge truth.
Kopia, lazygit, KDE Connect/GSConnect and Zellij remain optional and cannot displace the selected first-path tools without evidence.

metadata.source_request: Evaluate every interesting utility tool and register the resulting plan in Bureau.
validation: Infra PR #107 GitHub CI validate passed.
scripts/utilities/heim-utilities-smoke.sh reported Backrest, Beszel, Stirling PDF, Paperless, Atuin, Difftastic, rga and LocalSend OK.
Restic util-base-v1 snapshot and single-file restore probe passed.
Paperless imported synthetic PDF document id 1 and indexed content containing UTIL-BASE-V1.
Stirling PDF converted the probe PDF to text and merged two copies into a 2-page PDF.
Docling 2.111.0 converted the probe PDF to Markdown and JSON with UTIL-BASE-V1 content.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in infra PR #107 and live-smoked on heim-pc; see runtime evidence for exact boundaries and remaining manual steps.

verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t012-1: Runtime evidence and runbook record parked-tool boundaries.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t012-2: n8n remains deleted/not active and requires explicit later user decision before reopening.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t012-4: Kopia/lazygit/KDE Connect/GSConnect/Zellij remain optional and not selected first-path tools.
